method: read-only review of committed artifacts; no re-scoring, no fabrication
thesis: detection-F1 ranking does not predict re-identification-protection ranking on the deterministic national-ID channel

generalization.uniform_signal: GLiNER highest detection F1 (0.81-0.86) in every language yet leaks 36.4-100% of national IDs; kp-deid leaks 0% (4.07% only in RO legal domain)

weaknesses_flagged: cross-language claim rests on only 3 models present in all 11 (gliner, gliner2, kp-deid)
single authored template family in every language except RO; small n (~224-232) outside RO/PL
headline 'comparable detection F1' is false: kp-deid is F1 leader in zero languages; reframe as ranking dissociation
kp-deid not literally 0% in legal domain (4.07%, Wilson UB 0.0519)

paper_worthiness.verdict: YES, conditionally — strengthens Papers 1/3 generalization claim with tightened scope
paper_worthiness.scoped_claim: Across 11 EU decode-bearing national IDs, detection-F1 ranking does not predict re-id-protection ranking: highest-F1 model GLiNER leaks 36-100% of national IDs while kp-deid leaks ~0% (4.1% in RO legal). First *unified* multi-language demonstration.
mandatory_caveats: config_status=dev; NOT SOTA/citable; gated on RES-77 (native-speaker/IAA) and RES-53 (full-model re-score) before any public/citable use
11-language claim scoped to models scored per language; only RO/PL/IT/legal have all 8
re-identification reserved for national-ID channel; name/QI = residual distinctiveness, no k-anonymity (KLU-122)
kp-deid is ~0% not literally 0% in legal (4.07%)
